title: Day 1: Intro to Video Editing
content: Basics of the craft and software overview.
exercise: Install a video editing software (DaVinci Resolve or Premiere Pro) and import your first clip.

title: Day 2: Interface & Organization
content: How to set up your workspace and organize assets.
exercise: Create a folder structure for a new project: Footage, Audio, Assets, Renders.

title: Day 3: The Timeline Basics
content: Cutting, trimming, and moving clips.
exercise: Add 3 clips to the timeline and perform simple J-cuts and L-cuts.

title: Day 4: Audio Basics
content: Levelling and cleaning up sound.
exercise: Balance the background music with a voiceover track so the voice is clear.

title: Day 5: Transitions Done Right
content: When and how to use cuts, fades, and wipes.
exercise: Edit a 30-second sequence using only 'invisible' cuts.

title: Day 6: Color Correction Basics
content: Fixing exposure and white balance.
exercise: Correct the colors of a clip that looks too blue or too yellow.

title: Day 7: Simple Titles & Text
content: Adding information and style with text.
exercise: Create an intro title and lower thirds for a speaker.

title: Day 8: Speed Ramping
content: Using slow motion and fast motion for energy.
exercise: Apply a speed ramp to a clip of someone walking or a vehicle moving.

title: Day 9: B-Roll Mastery
content: Using secondary footage to tell a better story.
exercise: Edit a talking head video and overlay relevant B-roll clips.

title: Day 10: Sound Design & SFX
content: Enhancing the atmosphere with sound effects.
exercise: Add whooshes to transitions and ambient sounds to a landscape shot.

title: Day 11: Masking Basics
content: Isolating areas of your video for effects.
exercise: Mask out a moving object and change the color of the background.

title: Day 12: Keyframing Basics
content: Animating properties over time.
exercise: Animate a zoom-in on a photo over 5 seconds.

title: Day 13: Multi-cam Editing
content: Editing footage from multiple cameras.
exercise: Sync 2 audio/video sources and switch between them in a project.

title: Day 14: Green Screen (Chroma Key)
content: Replacing backgrounds efficiently.
exercise: Remove a green background and replace it with a studio backdrop.

title: Day 15: Exporting for Different Platforms
content: Codecs, resolutions, and aspect ratios.
exercise: Export the same video in 16:9 (YouTube) and 9:16 (TikTok) formats.

title: Day 16: Advanced Color Grading
content: Creating a 'look' using LUTs and wheels.
exercise: Create a 'cinematic' look for a beach scene.

title: Day 17: Motion Graphics Intro
content: Simple animations in your editor.
exercise: Animate a call-to-action button (Subscribe/Follow).

title: Day 18: Storytelling & Rhythm
content: The psychology of the edit.
exercise: Edit a 1-minute video that builds tension and then releases it.

title: Day 19: Working with Subtitles
content: Making your content accessible and engaging.
exercise: Auto-generate and style captions for a 1-minute video.

title: Day 20: Advanced Sound Mixing
content: Equalization, Compression, and Limiting.
exercise: Mix a project with voice, music, and SFX to industry standards (-3dB peak).

title: Day 21: Effects & Plugins
content: Beyond the built-in tools.
exercise: Apply an external plugin or template to your project.

title: Day 22: Proxy Workflow
content: Editing high-res footage on a low-end computer.
exercise: Generate proxies for a 4K clip and toggle between them.

title: Day 23: Vertical Video Masterclass
content: Specific techniques for Reels and TikTok.
exercise: Re-edit a horizontal video into a dynamic vertical video.

title: Day 24: Archiving & Project Management
content: How to save projects for the long term.
exercise: Archive a finished project including all linked media.

title: Day 25: Working with Clients
content: Feedback cycles and revision management.
exercise: Mock up a versioning system (v1, v2, Final_v1, etc.) for a project.

title: Day 26: AI Tools in Video Editing
content: Using AI for cutting, filling, and sound.
exercise: Use an AI tool (like Adobe Podcast or Topaz) to enhance a low-quality clip.

title: Day 27: Finding Your Style
content: Developing a consistent visual voice.
exercise: Analyze your favorite editor and list 3 specific techniques they use.

title: Day 28: Portfolio Building
content: How to show your work to get hired.
exercise: Draft the layout for your video editing showreel.

title: Day 29: Final Project: The Showreel
content: Creating your best work yet.
exercise: Edit a 60-second showreel using everything you've learned.

title: Day 30: The Journey Ahead
content: Staying relevant in an evolving field.
exercise: Set a goal to master one advanced technique (e.g., 3D tracking) next month.
